skill.name: canvas-design
skill.description: Guides AI assistants through creating a visual design philosophy and expressing it as a polished PNG or PDF canvas with sparse typography, structured composition, and bundled font choices.
skill.summary: Create refined visual design philosophies and turn them into polished PNG or PDF canvas artwork.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: The high static score is mainly false positives from bundled SIL Open Font License text and repeated design wording in SKILL.md. No malicious code, prompt injection attempt, credential access, or data exfiltration was found, but SKILL.md instructs the agent to download fonts as needed, which creates a legitimate network-use risk for a design skill.

medium_findings.title: External Font Download Instruction
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md tells the agent to download and use whatever fonts are needed. This is not malicious by itself, but it can cause network access to external font sources and should be disclosed or constrained to trusted sources.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction explicitly authorizes downloading external fonts. This is a real network-use risk, but no evidence shows exfiltration, command injection, or malicious endpoints.

low_findings.title: Font License Text Misclassified as Malware Indicators
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged hardcoded URLs, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance terms in Open Font License files. The reviewed lines are license metadata and legal definitions, not executable code or operational instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed files contain standard SIL Open Font License text and font project attribution. The same static patterns repeat across bundled license files with no executable behavior.

low_findings.title: Local Font Directory Search Misclassified as External Command Risk
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged SKILL.md line 108 as Ruby or shell backtick execution because it references the local ./canvas-fonts directory. The line is prose instructing the agent to look for bundled fonts, not shell syntax or a command execution primitive.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 108 contains a Markdown inline path reference and no executable code. The skill package has no script file at that location, so command execution is not supported by the evidence.

low_findings.title: Asserted User Feedback in Skill Instructions
low_findings.description: SKILL.md states that the user already requested a more perfect result. This can bias the agent toward an extra refinement pass, but it does not request secret access, privilege changes, or security-review bypass.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The statement is semantically suspicious because it injects assumed user feedback. Confidence is moderate because the intent appears quality-focused rather than malicious.

content.value_statement: Static visual requests often produce generic posters or text-heavy layouts. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create a design philosophy first, then render polished PNG or PDF artwork.
actual_capabilities: Creates a named visual design philosophy before producing the final artwork.
Guides output toward PNG or PDF canvas files with minimal supporting Markdown.
Emphasizes sparse typography, visual hierarchy, spacing, composition, and color systems.
Uses bundled font files from the canvas-fonts directory for typographic variety.
Supports single-page outputs by default and multi-page visual sets when requested.
Encourages subtle conceptual references instead of literal or text-heavy explanations.
limitations: Designed for static visual artifacts, not interactive applications or animations.
May request external font downloads unless constrained to bundled fonts.
Does not guarantee brand compliance unless brand rules are provided.
Cannot copy existing artists or copyrighted styles directly.

use_cases.title: Create Event Posters
use_cases.description: Turn a short event brief into a polished poster with expressive composition and restrained text.

use_cases.title: Explore Brand Art Direction
use_cases.description: Generate a visual philosophy and sample canvas for a campaign, product mood, or editorial concept.

use_cases.title: Produce Abstract Editorial Art
use_cases.description: Create a refined PNG or PDF visual that embeds a subtle topic reference without heavy explanation.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Poster
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a polished one-page poster for [topic]. Use the canvas-design process and keep the text minimal.

prompt_templates.title: Campaign Mood Study
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a visual philosophy for [campaign or product], then express it as a PNG or PDF canvas with refined typography.

prompt_templates.title: Subtle Reference Artwork
prompt_templates.scenario: Abstract artwork with hidden subject matter
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a museum-quality canvas about [subject]. Make the reference subtle, sophisticated, and mostly visual.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Page Visual Series
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a multi-page PDF visual series for [theme]. Keep one coherent philosophy, but make each page distinct.

output_examples.input: Create a poster for an experimental jazz night.
output_examples.output: A concise design philosophy file and a single-page poster with sparse type, rhythmic geometry, and a limited color palette.

output_examples.input: Make abstract art about a science fiction novel.
output_examples.output: A polished PNG or PDF canvas that uses spatial systems, subtle symbols, and minimal labels to imply the story world.

output_examples.input: Build a visual direction for a boutique ceramics studio.
output_examples.output: A named aesthetic movement, followed by a restrained canvas using texture, negative space, and quiet typography.

best_practices: Provide the subject, desired output format, page count, and any required text.
Constrain font use to bundled fonts when network access is not desired.
Share brand colors, logo rules, and print dimensions before final rendering.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to imitate a living artist or copyrighted visual style.
Do not use it for text-heavy documents that need dense explanations.
Do not leave output size, format, or required copy unspecified for production work.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a visual design philosophy and expresses it as a static PNG or PDF canvas.

faq.question: Can it make multi-page PDFs?
faq.answer: Yes. It defaults to one page, but it includes guidance for multi-page visual series.

faq.question: Does it use custom fonts?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a canvas-fonts directory and asks the agent to choose fonts for the artwork.

faq.question: Does it require internet access?
faq.answer: Not always. The skill includes fonts, but it also allows downloading fonts unless you restrict that behavior.

faq.question: Is it suitable for production brand work?
faq.answer: It can support early visual direction. Provide exact brand rules before using outputs in production.

faq.question: Can it copy a famous artist style?
faq.answer: No. The skill explicitly asks for original visual designs and avoids copying existing artists.
